CLI imports core via the declared ./server export with @kuzo-mcp/core already a dependency; PluginProcess ctor change is contained to its single call site; the new audit action is added consistently to both the union and the partition. Only two minor non-blocking advisories."}
-->

## 🏗️ Architecture Review

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 2

Clean from an architecture standpoint. Everything in my lane checks out:

**Package boundaries ✓** — `serve.ts` reaches core through `await import("@kuzo-mcp/core/server")`, and that subpath is now declared in `packages/core/package.json` `exports`. `@kuzo-mcp/core` is already a `dependency` of `@kuzo-mcp/cli`. No deep `dist/`/`src/` imports.

**Contract compliance ✓** — The `PluginProcess` constructor signature change (`capabilities: CredentialCapability[]` → `declaredCapabilities: {required, optional}`, `scopedEnv` now mutable) has exactly **one** call site (`loader.ts:329`), and `test:parity` exercises the full IPC round-trip. `runningProcesses()` returns a fresh copy with a documented "don't mutate" contract. The new `credential.refreshed_in_flight` audit action is added to **both** the `AuditAction` union (`audit.ts`) and the `AUDIT_ACTION_PARTITION` record (`audit-partition.ts`) as `parent-only` — consistent, and the `Record<AuditAction, …>` would have failed typecheck otherwise.

**File organization ✓** — `serve.ts` sits with the other CLI commands; `debounce.ts` lives under `credentials/`; `serve-summary.ts` is a pure, unit-tested helper in core. No god files, no inline handlers, no premature abstraction.

**No cross-plugin imports** — no plugin packages touched.

**`kuzo serve` registration ✓** — verified `claudeCodeAlreadyWired()` (post-install.ts:131) already recognizes `command: "kuzo"`, so the new canonical settings block introduced here is detected by the existing onboarding check. No follow-up needed.

### Advisories (non-blocking)

1. **Duplicated capability-split shape** (`plugin-process.ts:151`, `loader.ts`, `source.ts:67`) — same `{required, optional}` structural type inline in three modules, with a readonly-vs-mutable variance. A shared named type would prevent drift. Inline comment posted.
2. **Redundant `./server` export** (`core/package.json:22`) — identical to the existing `.` export (both → `dist/server.js`). `@kuzo-mcp/core` (bare) would already resolve `runServer`. Harmless and arguably intentional (an explicit, semantically-named entry that decouples from whatever `.` means later) — just noting the redundancy.

The §C.11 deviation flagged in the PR description (`refreshCredentials` also updating stored `scopedEnv` for lazy/respawn children, beyond the spec's notify-only snippet) is a sound, well-documented correctness improvement and fits cleanly within the existing parent/child IPC architecture.

---

Idempotent + basename filter make it low-severity."}],"rationale":"Type contracts all check out (RunServerOptions.scrub, ./server export, extractForPlugin signature vs declaredCapabilities split, credentialStore.isUnlocked/reload/size, IPC notify/onNotification, collectEnvOverrides returns Record so Object.keys is valid). No any, type-only imports correct, .js suffixes present, floating promise properly void+catch'd. Only one onNotification handler in plugin-host so no handler clobber. Single low-severity advisory on the debounce semantics."}
-->

## ✅ Correctness Review

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 1
**CI status**: green (both `CI` and `Claude Auto Review` runs still `in_progress` at review time — neither failing)

### Summary

Clean, well-scoped change. The §C.11 rotation path and §D.3 serve-summary wiring type-check correctly and respect the project conventions. Verified the load-bearing contracts:

- **`runServer({ scrub })`** — `RunServerOptions.scrub` exists and `--no-scrub` → `options.scrub === false` is handled correctly (Commander negated-flag default `true`). The `KUZO_DEV` gate + refuse-to-boot guard is sound.
- **`@kuzo-mcp/core/server`** subpath export added in `package.json` matches the dynamic `import()` in `serve.ts`.
- **`extractForPlugin(proc.declaredCapabilities)`** — the new required/optional split on `PluginProcess.declaredCapabilities` matches `CredentialSource.extractForPlugin`'s param shape; `CredentialCapability[]` → `readonly CredentialCapability[]` assignability is fine.
- **IPC** — `notify` / `onNotification` exist; `refreshCredentials` correctly guards `channel && state === "ready"`. Only one `onNotification` slot is used in `plugin-host.ts`, so the new `credential.refresh` handler doesn't clobber an existing one.
- **`credentialStore`** — `isUnlocked()`, `reload()`, `size` all present; watcher only installed when unlocked.
- **`buildServeSummary`** — `noCredsAnywhere` and the R35 migrate nudge are mutually exclusive (zero vs. >0 env overrides); `Object.keys(envOverrides)` is valid since `collectEnvOverrides` returns a `Record<string,string>`.
- No `any`, type-only imports correct (`import type`, inline `type FSWatcher`), all relative ESM imports carry `.js`, the watcher's floating promise is properly `void`-ed with a `.catch`.

### Advisory (non-blocking)

1. **`server.ts:397`** — `debounce(250)` delays per-event rather than coalescing; a multi-event rotation could trigger N reloads / IPC pushes / audit events. Idempotent and largely mitigated by the basename filter, so low-severity. See inline comment.

No secret leakage - summary and audit log credential names and counts only, never values. --no-scrub is gated behind KUZO_DEV with a hard refusal and exit 80, and scrub_disabled is audited. IPC credential.refresh is a trusted parent-to-child channel. No supply-chain, workflow, or publishConfig changes. One advisory on the rotation watcher null-filename edge." }
-->

## Security Review

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 1

Theme 9 wires `kuzo serve` + C.11 rotation cache invalidation + D.3 first-run UX. The credential-handling surface is the security-relevant part and it holds up well.

### What is solid

- **No secret leakage.** `serve-summary.ts` prints plugin names, skip reasons, and credential *names* (GITHUB_TOKEN, JIRA_*) plus counts - never values. `envOverrideNames` is used only for length branching, never interpolated into output. The `credential.refreshed_in_flight` audit logs `count_refreshed` only; the reload-error branch logs `err.message` (decryption/tamper messages, not plaintext).
- **--no-scrub is properly gated.** `serve.ts` refuses to boot (exit 80 E_SERVER_BOOT_FAILED) unless KUZO_DEV=1 or true, so a stray flag in a non-interactive settings.json launch (env emptied) cannot silently let plugin children inherit credential env vars. `credential.scrub_disabled` is audited (server.ts:348) when the escape hatch is used. Good defense-in-depth.
- **IPC credential.refresh is parent-to-child (trusted).** `plugin-host.ts` defensively type-guards the incoming config before swapping. The refresh path does not widen any trust boundary - children already receive their scoped creds at spawn.
- **No supply-chain movement.** No dependency/lockfile changes, no release.yml/OIDC/Sigstore touch, no .changeset, no postinstall. `packages/core/package.json` adds only a `./server` subpath export pointing at the already-published `dist/server.js` - no new publish surface.
- **Watcher lifecycle is sound** - unref-ed, closed before store teardown, only installed when the store actually unlocked, and the dir-watch+basename approach survives the atomic tmp+rename rotation.

### Advisory

- **server.ts:442 - null filename rotation events are silently dropped.** `fs.watch` does not guarantee `filename`; a null event fails the basename check and the rotation is never applied. The security-relevant case is *revocation* - a leaked-then-rotated credential keeps being served to live children until restart (fail-stale). Suggested: also reload when filename is null. Non-blocking; flagging so the deferred live-rotation QA smoke covers it. (Inline comment posted.)

---

sensitive_paths_touched: true - the diff materially changes the credential-propagation path (credentials.ts, plugin-host.ts IPC, plugin-process.ts, loader.ts) even though no file matches the literal sensitive-path globs. Erring toward true for a credential-handling change.

## 📋 Auto-Review Verdict — Round 3

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 3
**CI**: green — all checks pass (Build, Typecheck, Lint, Install parity, Provenance smoke, CI, Label)

### Specialists
- 🔒 Security: ship (0 blocking, 1 advisory)
- 🏗️ Architecture: ship (0 blocking, 1 advisory)
- ✅ Correctness: ship (0 blocking, 1 advisory)

### Top action items
- [ ] Credential dir-watch can self-perpetuate on null-filename platforms: the refresh handler's own `credential.refreshed_in_flight` write to `audit.log` (same `~/.kuzo` dir) re-triggers the watch in a ~250ms reload/audit/reload loop. Linux always supplies `filename` so it doesn't fire there; gate the null-filename reload behind an mtime/size stat or move the store to its own subdir — `packages/core/src/server.ts:478`
- [ ] Stale comment: references a `refreshPending` guard, but the actual coalescing variables are `refreshing`/`rerun` — one-word fix when convenient — `packages/core/src/server.ts:398`
- [ ] Redundant `./server` subpath export — resolves byte-for-byte to the same module as the root `.` export, so `runServer` is reachable two ways; defensible per §D.1 as the canonical named entry, just noting the API-surface ambiguity — `packages/core/package.json:22`

> ✅ Round-2 items resolved: trailing-edge re-arm (`refreshing`/`rerun`) now picks up a distinct rotation landing in the sub-ms window rather than dropping it, closing the fail-stale gap without re-introducing N reloads per burst (`ba3667e`).

---

## 🏗️ Architecture Review

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 1

Clean from the architecture lane. Everything lines up with the locked plugin/monorepo design.

**Contract & boundaries — all pass**
- `kuzo serve` reaches the server only through the package's public API: CLI's `package.json` declares `@kuzo-mcp/core` (`workspace:^`) and `serve.ts` does `await import("@kuzo-mcp/core/server")`. No deep import into `dist/`/`src/`. `runServer({ scrub })` matches `RunServerOptions.scrub?: boolean`.
- `DeclaredCredentialCapabilities` is centralized in `credentials/source.ts`, re-exported via the `./credentials` barrel (`index.ts`), and imported by both `loader.ts` and `plugin-process.ts` through `./credentials/index.js` — the right move to prevent the required-vs-optional split drifting across call sites.
- `PluginProcess` constructor signature change (flat `capabilities: CredentialCapability[]` → split `declaredCapabilities`) is an internal contract; the loader is the sole caller and is updated correctly. No leftover stale references — `loader.ts:370` `capabilities: plugin.capabilities` is the unrelated proxy-manifest reconstruction, not the ctor.
- The §C.11 wiring is coherent: `loader.runningProcesses()` → `proc.refreshCredentials()` → `credentialSource.extractForPlugin(proc.declaredCapabilities)` → IPC `credential.refresh` → broker `replaceConfigAtomically`. New `credential.refreshed_in_flight` audit action is correctly partitioned `parent-only`.

**File organization & anti-patterns — clean**
- `serve-summary.ts` (pure, unit-tested) and `debounce.ts` (tiny helper) are properly extracted; the `fs.watch` lifecycle stays inline in `runServer` rather than being wrapped in a premature abstraction — correct per "start simple."
- No cross-plugin imports, no god file (largest add is `server.ts` +109), no new singletons / DI / hot-reload framework.

**Advisory (low):** `packages/core/package.json` — the new `./server` export resolves to the exact same `dist/server.js` + `dist/server.d.ts` as the root `.` export, so `@kuzo-mcp/core` and `@kuzo-mcp/core/server` are interchangeable today. Defensible as an explicit named entry (and future-proofs the root becoming a barrel), just flagging it as redundant public surface — not blocking.

---

**✅ Correctness Review**

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 / **Advisory**: 0
**CI status**: green - all checks pass (Build, Typecheck, Lint, Install parity, Provenance smoke, CI, Label)

Reviewed Theme 9 against the correctness lane. Clean - nothing to block or flag.

**What I verified**

_kuzo serve (serve.ts / index.ts)_
- The --no-scrub dev-gate is sound: Commander negated flag means options.scrub defaults true and --no-scrub sets false; the refusal path exits 80 (E_SERVER_BOOT_FAILED) and the boot-failure catch reuses the same code. runServer maps it to doScrub = options.scrub is-not false.
- The async action is correctly awaited by Commander; the dynamic import of the core/server subpath plus awaited runServer - no floating promise.

_C.11 rotation watch (server.ts / plugin-process.ts / plugin-host.ts / credentials.ts)_
- Watcher install is correctly gated on credentialStore.isUnlocked() AFTER loadAll() (step 10 then 11b), so the env-override-only / cold-store no-op holds.
- Store API used by the watcher all exists and behaves: size (returns 0 when never unlocked), isUnlocked(), reload().
- Burst coalescing is correct: the refreshPending guard plus the 250ms debounce collapse the tmp+rename event burst into one reload; the trailing reload reads post-write disk state so the latest rotation value is captured. The watch callback voids the promise chain with catch and finally - no floating promise, the flag resets cleanly.
- The dirname/basename watch-the-directory approach survives the atomic-rename inode swap; a null filename fail-safe reloads rather than dropping a revocation.
- IPC notification handler narrows params from unknown (no any); the typeof-object guard correctly rejects undefined and missing config.
- refreshCredentials updates this.scopedEnv AND notifies only when state is ready; spawn() re-reads this.scopedEnv on each fork (line 197-198), so lazy/respawn children boot with rotated values. The ctor-signature change (capabilities to declaredCapabilities) is consistent across loader/process and confirmed by the parity test.

_D.3 serve-summary_
- Pure and branch-correct: the zero-creds-anywhere case and the R35 nudge are mutually exclusive; the first-run two-liner suppresses the generic set-hint. The test matrix covers all branches.

_In-lane checklist_
- No any - IPC params narrowed from unknown. PASS
- import type on type-only imports. PASS
- ESM .js suffix on every relative import; node builtins exempt. PASS
- Nullish coalescing used for KUZO_NO_MIGRATE_NUDGE. PASS
- No floating promises / silent error-swallowing; error paths log and audit. PASS
- Error messages actionable (Claude/human-readable), no stack traces. PASS
- No console.log / debugger / orphan TODO. PASS
- No unused imports/dead code - extractCredentialCapabilities and its import removed cleanly; isCredentialCapability still used. PASS

One narrow advisory on double-rotation coalescing."}

**Security Review**

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 1

Reviewed against the Phase 2.5/2.6 credential threat model. This is a careful, security-aware PR. The kuzo serve surface and the C.11 rotation path both hold up.

**What is solid (no action needed)**

- **--no-scrub gate (serve.ts:64)** refuses to boot with exit 80 unless KUZO_DEV=1|true, so disabling env scrubbing can never be a stray-flag footgun in a non-interactive settings.json launch. Good defense-in-depth. ALWAYS_SCRUB still strips KUZO_PASSPHRASE even when scrub is disabled (server.ts:341).
- **Rotation precedence is consistent.** The C.11 watcher re-resolves via credentialSource.extractForPlugin then get, which applies the same env-override-wins precedence as boot (source.ts:46). So a rotation cannot invert precedence or strip an env-shadowed credential. The deferred rotate-3x QA item is a test-environment shadowing limitation, not a correctness gap.
- **Tamper/corruption safety (server.ts:821-835).** A bad in-flight reload is caught, audited (credential.refreshed_in_flight / error), and the prior decrypted cache is retained. The watcher never crashes the server on a malformed or tampered store.
- **No secret leakage.** The D.3 summary surfaces credential names only (Object.keys(envOverrides), storeSize), never values, and writes to stderr (stdout is the MCP transport). The credential.refresh IPC payload carries values, but only parent-to-child over the existing trusted fork channel.
- **Publish surface unchanged.** packages/core/package.json adds a ./server exports subpath (consumed by the CLI). No publishConfig, OIDC, or provenance drift.
- **Fail-safe watch on null filename (server.ts:868).** A null filename reloads rather than dropping, so a revocation-driven rotation is never silently missed on platforms that omit the filename.

**Advisory**

- **server.ts:862 burst-coalescing can drop a rotation in a narrow window.** refreshPending is reset in the finally block after handleCredentialFileChange completes its reload. A second rotation whose watch event arrives between the reload read and the finally reset is dropped and never re-read, so its new value will not propagate until the next unrelated watch event. The window is sub-millisecond and a single revocation (one atomic tmp+rename) is fully covered, since the debounced handler reads the latest on-disk state, so this only affects two genuinely concurrent rotations. Low risk; flagging because revocation propagation is in-scope. A trailing-edge re-check (re-arm if an event arrived while in-flight) would close it if you want belt-and-suspenders.

---

Async hygiene, nullish handling, store/IPC API usage, and the rotation-watcher coalescing logic all check out. Sole nit is a stale variable name in one explanatory comment."}
-->

## ✅ Correctness Review

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 1
**CI status**: green — Build, Lint, Typecheck, Install parity, Provenance smoke all passing.

Verified the load-bearing claims of this diff rather than trusting the prose:

- **IPC contract** — `IpcChannel.onNotification` (single handler) / `notify` exist (`ipc.ts:153,185`). `plugin-host.ts:142` is the *only* `onNotification` registration, so the new `credential.refresh` handler clobbers nothing. The handler's `credentialBroker`/`pluginContext` are in scope (`let` at `plugin-host.ts:104-105`) and null-guarded before use.
- **Null handling** — the handler's `config === null || typeof config !== "object"` guard is correct precisely because `typeof null === "object"`; the explicit null check is load-bearing, not redundant. `??` used throughout (`server.ts:512`, `serve-summary.ts`).
- **Store API** — `reload()` / `isUnlocked()` / `size` (getter) / `close()` all exist on `EncryptedCredentialStore` (`store.ts:127-133,239`). `extractForPlugin` reads `this.get()` *live* (`source.ts:84-95`), so `reload()` is reflected on the next re-resolve — the refresh path is sound.
- **Async hygiene** — `handleCredentialFileChange` is a properly-floated promise (`void …catch().finally()`), the watch callback is sync, and the leading-edge/`rerun` trailing-edge coalescing is correct (exactly one extra refresh after an in-flight burst). `watcher.unref()` + shutdown `watcher?.close()` are wired.
- **Commander negated flag** — `--no-scrub` → `options.scrub: boolean` typed correctly; the `KUZO_DEV` gate refuses boot with a Claude-readable stderr message and a real exit code (`E_SERVER_BOOT_FAILED`). No stack traces leaked to tool output.
- No `any`, no unjustified non-null assertions, no `console.*`/`debugger`/`TODO` in the new files.

### Advisory (non-blocking)

- `server.ts:398` — the comment says burst coalescing is *"done by the `refreshPending` guard in the watch callback below"*, but the actual variables are `refreshing` / `rerun` (`server.ts:451-468`). There is no `refreshPending`. Harmless today, mildly misleading for the next maintainer — worth a one-word fix when convenient.

## Security Review

**Verdict**: ship
**Blocking**: 0 | **Advisory**: 1

The security-critical surface of this PR is solid.

### What is correct (no action)

- **--no-scrub gate** (packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts:64, packages/core/src/server.ts:267): fail-safe by construction. doScrub = options.scrub !== false defaults scrubbing ON; kuzo serve with no flag becomes scrub:true. The CLI refuses to boot (exit 80) unless KUZO_DEV=1|true, and even with scrub disabled KUZO_PASSPHRASE / KUZO_NO_ENV_SCRUB are still removed via ALWAYS_SCRUB. credential.scrub_disabled is audited without double-counting. Closes the stray-flag-in-a-non-interactive-settings.json-launch footgun.
- **credential.refresh IPC** (plugin-host.ts:462, plugin-process.ts:545, server.ts:430): trust direction is parent-to-child only. The parent re-resolves each plugin via credentialSource.extractForPlugin(proc.declaredCapabilities), so a child only ever receives its own already-scoped credential set. No cross-plugin leak or escalation. Handler null/type-checks the payload before swapping config.
- **replaceConfigAtomically** (credentials.ts:229): clears clientCache so the next getClient rebuilds with the rotated value; stale-stashed-client limitation documented per spec (locked design).
- **credential.refreshed_in_flight**: correctly classified parent-only in audit-partition.ts:53; a compromised child emitting it is rewritten to audit.forged_action at the IPC boundary.
- **No secret leakage**: serve-summary.ts emits plugin names, skip reasons, env-override names (Object.keys), and counts only, never values. Watcher logs paths only.
- **Supply chain**: no dependency/lockfile changes, no publishConfig change, the new ./server subpath export only re-points to the existing runServer, no workflow edits.

### Advisory

**packages/core/src/server.ts:478 -- credential watcher can self-perpetuate on null-filename platforms.**

The watch is on dirname(credentials.enc) = ~/.kuzo, which also holds the frequently-written audit.log. The basename filter (filename !== null AND filename !== credBasename returns) correctly drops sibling-file events when filename is provided (Linux inotify always does). But on the fail-safe path (filename === null) the handler reloads on any change in the dir, and handleCredentialFileChange itself writes credential.refreshed_in_flight to audit.log. That write fires the watch again (null filename, reload, audit write, repeat), and the trailing-edge rerun re-arm keeps the cycle going: a permanent ~250ms reload/decrypt/IPC-broadcast loop.

This does not fire on Linux (filename always present) and is unlikely on macOS, so it is an availability edge, not a secret/auth issue -- advisory, not blocking. The inline note that a spurious reload is cheap overlooks that the reloads own audit write is what re-triggers it. Cheap mitigations: on null filename, gate the reload behind an fs.stat mtime/size check against the last-seen credential-file stat (no-op when unchanged), or keep the credential store in its own subdirectory so audit.log writes cannot reach the watch.
